introSection: Tigris is a steel launched roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. The ride was manufactured by Premier Rides and opened on April 19, 2019. It was the tallest launched roller coaster in Florida until VelociCoaster at Universal's Islands of Adventure opened in 2021.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In March 2018, SeaWorld Parks & Entertainment filed three trademark applications for the names Tigris, Uproar, and Twisted Tiger. Tigris was announced on September 12, 2018. Vertical construction began in December.

In February 2019, the track layout was completed. Testing then began the following month.

On April 4, 2019, Busch Gardens Tampa confirmed that Tigris would open on April 19, 2019. The ride soft opened on April 16 and April 17, however.

The ride's "comfort collars" were removed in December 2025.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Tigris is a Sky Rocket II from Premier Rides, similar to Tempesto at Busch Gardens Williamsburg and Electric Eel at SeaWorld San Diego. The ride has orange track and black supports and replaced Tanganyika Tidal Wave, a shoot-the-chutes attraction that closed in 2016.

fullText:
Tigris is a steel launched roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. The ride was manufactured by Premier Rides and opened on April 19, 2019. It was the tallest launched roller coaster in Florida until VelociCoaster at Universal's Islands of Adventure opened in 2021.

In March 2018, SeaWorld Parks & Entertainment filed three trademark applications for the names Tigris, Uproar, and Twisted Tiger. Tigris was announced on September 12, 2018. Vertical construction began in December.

In February 2019, the track layout was completed. Testing then began the following month.

On April 4, 2019, Busch Gardens Tampa confirmed that Tigris would open on April 19, 2019. The ride soft opened on April 16 and April 17, however.

The ride's "comfort collars" were removed in December 2025.

Tigris is a Sky Rocket II from Premier Rides, similar to Tempesto at Busch Gardens Williamsburg and Electric Eel at SeaWorld San Diego. The ride has orange track and black supports and replaced Tanganyika Tidal Wave, a shoot-the-chutes attraction that closed in 2016.
